Title: Product Rearrangement from Altering a Single Residue in the Rice syn-Copalyl Diphosphate Synthase

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II diterpene cyclase; copalyl diphosphate synthase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors of specialized labdane-related diterpenoids including rice allelochemicals momilactones and antifungal phytoalexins; syn-HPP is suggested precursor to medicinal diterpenoids from Vitex species
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: syn-CPP branch of diterpene cyclization; rearranged syn-HPP side branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream committed cyclization step from GGPP to bicyclic diphosphate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: syn-copalyl diphosphate (wild type); syn-halima-5,13E-dienyl diphosphate in H501 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes protonation-initiated bicyclization of GGPP to syn-copalyl diphosphate; mutation at H501 redirects the carbocation cascade to rearranged syn-halima-5,13E-dienyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms diterpene pathway branch-point precursor/product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type OsCPS4 produces syn-CPP, while single-residue substitutions at H501 induce product rearrangement and formation of syn-HPP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme for the syn-labdane diterpenoid branch; key control point for product skeleton outcome established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in engineered E. coli producing GGPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous expression in engineered E. coli; GC-MS of dephosphorylated products; purification and NMR structural identification; protein structure modeling; quantum chemical calculations
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: H501 substitutions reduced overall catalytic yield and left substantial unreacted GGPP-derived precursor pathway flux; some mutants produced mixed products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme target for metabolic engineering of novel labdane-related diterpenoid skeletons; single-residue editing can redirect product outcome from syn-CPP to syn-HPP
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS4 is the only demonstrated plant CPS producing syn-CPP. 2. Mutating H501 changes product outcome from syn-CPP to the novel rearranged syn-HPP. 3. OsCPS4 therefore controls diterpene skeleton formation and reveals high catalytic plasticity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: H251A and C310A did not alter product outcome; H275 substitutions had little effect; H501 occupies a position otherwise generally conserved as Tyr in plant DTCs.
